frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery (FLS) for Sinistral Learners' with production credits from UPMC
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational video introduction targeting left-handed learners

frame_analyses.description: Slide explaining context: 10% of physicians left-handed, deficiency in teaching dominant hand development, resource scarcity for left-handed trainees
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Establishes educational purpose and context for left-handed FLS instruction

frame_analyses.description: Peg transfer trainer visible with colored foam objects (orange, green, yellow) on pegs with black graspers
Pattern cut task showing gauze suspended between clamps with circular outline marked in black ink (4cm diameter, approximately)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task 2 setup with clear marking visible

frame_analyses.description: Scissors begin cutting along the marked circular line; initial entry at top of circle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perpendicular approach to gauze, smooth controlled motion initiating cut

frame_analyses.description: Continued cutting clockwise along circular outline; gauze remains stable on clamps, cut appears on-line
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth progression, no visible deviation from marked line

frame_analyses.description: Final segment of circular cut being completed; nearly full circumference cut visible
Endoloop task setup showing red foam appendage (three-pronged structure) with suture preparation visible
Educational slide showing 'Anatomy of the Needle' with labeled needle components (A=point, B=body, C=eye/attachment)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional diagram demonstrating proper needle anatomy and grasp positions for optimal technique

frame_analyses.description: Position A shown: Penrose drain secured on red felt pad; suture entry point marked; left-handed needle driver approach with arrow indicating proper grasping position for left-handed surgeon
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates correct needle grasp position for left-handed learner; suture marks clearly visible on drain

frame_analyses.description: Close-up showing needle driver grasping needle shaft and beginning to pass through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean grasp at approximately 2/3 point along needle; controlled entry angle

frame_analyses.description: Suture fully passed through both marked points on drain with appropriate spacing; suture loop visible above drain with knot pusher and suture configured for extracorporeal knot tying
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement appears centered on marked targets; adequate loop length for knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Final slide: 'Conclusion' stating key teaching points for left-handed learners and encouraging mentor support for dominant hand development
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational summary emphasizing inclusive surgical training for left-handed trainees

cannot_score_reason: This video is classified as INSTRUCTIONAL content. It is an educational tutorial with narrated slides, equipment demonstrations, and teaching slides (background, needle anatomy, conclusion) interspersed with task demonstrations. The video contains no formal timed performance of a complete FLS task by a trainee attempting to be scored.

demonstrated_techniques: Pattern cutting technique for circular excision with marked outline
Proper needle grasp position at 2/3 point from needle tip
Left-handed needle driver positioning and approach angles
Penrose drain suture placement through bilateral marks
Controlled cutting motion maintaining line fidelity
Peg transfer setup for bilateral hand coordination
verbal_cues: Video explicitly addresses left-handed learners (title slide)
Emphasizes deficiency in teaching left-handed dominant hand development
Encourages mentors to support both left and right-handed surgeons
Highlights resource scarcity for left-handed surgical training
common_errors_addressed: Difficulty left-handed learners have converting right-handed demonstrations to mirror image
Insufficient mentorship for dominant hand development in left-handed trainees
Lack of inclusive training environments for non-right-handed surgeons

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is certain based on clear title slides, educational context, narrated background information, anatomical teaching slides, and conclusion summary. This is definitively not a performance video attempting to achieve an FLS score.
scoring_note: This video does NOT qualify for FLS scoring. It is an educational resource demonstrating FLS techniques for left-handed learners. Per protocol, instructional videos are not scored; teaching content is extracted instead.
